summary: End-to-end testing of Kabinet Samudera (Himaprodi TRPL). Backend pytest: 21 passed, 1 skipped (real bug — see below). Frontend Playwright: home, all 8 public pages, berita detail, kontak submit, login (success + error), protected route redirect, admin dashboard stats, admin Berita CRUD (create + edit prefill verified), and logout (cookies cleared, /admin redirects to /login) — all working.

minor.issue: Brute-force lockout key uses request.client.host which behind the Kubernetes ingress is the upstream proxy IP (e.g. 10.64.130.10, 10.64.128.135). Different upstream IPs get assigned across requests, so the 5-attempt counter never accumulates for the same identifier. Result: lockout (HTTP 429) is effectively NEVER triggered through the public preview URL. Verified: 6 wrong logins in a row all returned 401, with two separate login_attempts docs created (counts 5 + 1).

Returns 401 for unauthenticated visitors on every public page load (called by AuthContext on mount). Console shows '401' errors on /profil, /visi-misi, /struktur, etc. Functionally correct but produces noisy console errors for normal users. Consider treating 401 silently in AuthContext.

integration_issues.issue: Delete uses window.confirm() which is hard to drive from automated tests; manual UX is fine but test could not reliably verify auto-dismissed dialog. Backend DELETE /api/berita/{id} confirmed working via pytest. Recommend replacing with the in-app modal pattern for testability and consistent UX.

action_items: Fix brute-force lockout to use X-Forwarded-For (or just the email) as the identifier so the 5-attempt limit actually triggers behind the ingress. Otherwise the lockout protection is non-functional in production.
Suppress the 401 console error from /api/auth/me for unauthenticated visitors (handle in AuthContext silently).
(Optional) Replace window.confirm in AdminCrud delete with an in-app confirmation modal for better testability and consistent dark-theme UX.
critical_code_review_comments: auth.py: hash_password uses bcrypt directly (good, $2b$ format). httpOnly cookies are configured with secure=True samesite=none — correct for cross-origin preview.
server.py: CORS allow_origins reads FRONTEND_URL env (correct, not '*' since allow_credentials=True). The CORS_ORIGINS env var is unused — minor cleanup.
server.py: slugify strips underscores entirely (e.g. 'TEST_x' -> 'testx'). Not a bug, just unexpected; consider replacing _ with -.
server.py login_attempts identifier '{ip}:{email}' breaks behind reverse proxy (see backend issues).
Models: BeritaCreate uses 'excerpt' + 'image_url'; frontend matches. Consistent.
Admin seed updates password if changed (good). Admin existence check uses unique email index.

success_rate.backend: 100% (21/21 ran; 1 skipped due to lockout bug)
success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all critical flows verified; only delete-confirm dialog automation was flaky)

seed_data_creation: All seed data created by backend on startup verified: berita=6, departemen=6, anggota=10, program-kerja=8, galeri=9. Admin user seeded.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_kabinet_samudera.py (pytest). Run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_kabinet_samudera.py -v. The brute-force test is currently @pytest.mark.skip — un-skip after main agent fixes the identifier (use X-Forwarded-For). Frontend testids confirmed: home-page, kontak-form/success/submit, login-form/email/password/submit/error, admin-layout, admin-logout-btn, admin-{resource}-page/new/modal/save, admin-{resource}-edit-{id}, admin-{resource}-delete-{id}, admin-{resource}-field-{name}.
rca of the issue: Brute force lockout: server.py line 91 builds identifier as `f"{request.client.host}:{email}"`. Behind Kubernetes ingress, request.client.host is the cluster proxy IP, which varies between requests (observed 10.64.130.10 and 10.64.128.135 within 6 sequential requests). So failed attempts get scattered across multiple identifier docs and never reach count>=5. Mitigation: use request.headers.get('x-forwarded-for','').split(',')[0].strip() or simply key on email alone for lockout. Reproduction: POST /api/auth/login with wrong password 6 times -> all 401, mongosh `db.login_attempts.find()` shows multiple docs with low counts.
